description: This figure presents X-ray diffraction patterns of Fe–28Mn–9Al-1.2C-xCr (x = 0, 3, 6, 9 wt%) austenitic low-density steel strips after annealing at 550 °C for 5 h. The x-axis represents 2θ ranging from 30° to 100°, and the y-axis is diffraction intensity in arbitrary units. Four stacked curves correspond to 0Cr (black), 3Cr (blue), 6Cr (red), and 9Cr (green) samples respectively. Identified phases include face-centered cubic austenite (γ, indexed to (111), (200), (220), (311), (222) peaks), body-centered cubic δ-ferrite (indexed to (110), (200), (211) peaks), and κ-carbide (κ(111) peak at ~41.4°). The κ-carbide peak is only observed in the 0Cr sample, while it is completely absent in all Cr-containing samples. All samples retain austenite and δ-ferrite as primary constituent phases after annealing. The results demonstrate that Cr addition effectively suppresses κ-carbide precipitation in this steel system even after the 550 °C 5 h annealing treatment.
